title: Hemionitis microphylla
taxonomy: Hemionitis microphylla is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This classification places Hemionitis microphylla within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The genus Hemionitis is a part of the order Polypodiales, which comprises many species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. The classification of Hemionitis microphylla is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its small size and delicate fronds. Hemionitis microphylla's classification is a testament to the complexities and diversities within the plant kingdom.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat wounds and skin infections. Its leaves are also used to treat fever and to reduce swelling. In traditional medicine, Hemionitis microphylla is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts are also being researched for their potential anti-cancer properties.
